title: Chris Boettcher
content:
People.ContactEmail cboettcher@mccarthy.ca
- University of Calgary
Chris Boettcher is an Associate in McCarthy Tétrault’s Labour & Employment Group, based in Calgary, Alberta. He advises employers on strategic workplace matters with a practical and solutions-oriented approach, helping clients manage legal risk while advancing business objectives.
Chris’s practice spans the full range of workplace issues, from day-to-day advisory work to complex disputes. He regularly advises employers on hiring, discipline, terminations, workplace investigations, accommodation, policy development, restrictive covenants, privacy, and other workplace risk management issues. Clients rely on Chris for guidance on a broad range of labour and employment matters, including employment agreements, executive compensation and incentive entitlements, wrongful dismissal, human rights, labour relations, occupational health and safety, workers’ compensation, and privacy matters. Likewise, Chris frequently advises U.S.-based employers with operations or employees in Alberta and has particular experience helping cross-border clients navigate the differences between Canadian and U.S. workplace laws and practices.
Clients also benefit from Chris’s deep advocacy experience. Chris has represented clients in wrongful dismissal actions, restrictive covenant disputes, labour arbitrations, workers’ compensation claims, human rights complaints, and a wide range of other employment disputes. Chris has represented clients in matters before the Court of King’s Bench of Alberta, the Alberta Court of Justice, the Court of King’s Bench for Saskatchewan, and the Federal Court, as well as numerous administrative boards and tribunals, including the Alberta Labour Relations Board, the Alberta Human Rights Tribunal, and workers’ compensation boards in both Alberta and Saskatchewan.
In addition, Chris routinely provides labour and employment advice in M&A transactions, including supporting McCarthy Tétrault deal teams on the following matters:
- Nippon Express Holdings in its acquisition of Metro Supply Chain Group Inc. from LDC Metro Holdings Inc. and CDP Investissements Inc. for C$2.2 billion
- The Carlyle Group in its structured capital investment in Peloton Computer Enterprises, a provider of integrated energy data management software
- International Paper in the sale of its Global Cellulose Fibers division to American Industrial Partners for US$1.5 billion
- Ares Management LLC and Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec in their co-investment in Synex Business Performance as minority shareholders
- National Bank of Canada in its acquisition of Canadian Western Bank for approximately C$5 billion
- Brookfield Renewable Holdings in its acquisition of a 53.12% stake in Neoen for €3.25 billion
Chris’s combined advisory, advocacy, and deal experience allows him to provide clients with commercially informed advice across the full lifecycle of the employment relationship.
Chris regularly also presents on labour and employment developments, including employee management, complex dismissal, contractor classification, leave management, and incentive compensation issues.
Chris received his J.D. from the University of Calgary, Faculty of Law, in 2021. He also holds a Bachelor of Arts, Honours (First Class), in Political Science from the University of Calgary. Prior to attending law school, Chris spent six years working for a major Canadian airline.
Chris is a member of the Law Society of Alberta, the Canadian Bar Association, and the Calgary Bar Association. He was called to the Alberta Bar in 2022.
- Nippon Express Holdings to acquire Metro Supply Chain for Up to C$2.2B April 17, 2026
- The Carlyle Group provides structured capital to Peloton Computer Enterprises February 23, 2026
- International Paper to sell Global Cellulose Fibers business to American Industrial Partners for US$1.5B December 23, 2025
- Ares Management LLC and Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec co-invest in Synex Business Performance April 3, 2025
- National Bank of Canada acquires Canadian Western Bank for approximately C$5B February 3, 2025
- Brookfield Renewable Holdings completes acquisition of 53.12% stake in Neoen for EUR 3.25B December 30, 2024
- Blog Post Proving Failure to Mitigate: Rare, but Not Impossible October 8, 2025
- Publication Mining in the Courts, Vol. XIV March 5, 2024
